preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced velar fricative (ɣ) to a voiced alveolo-palatal affricate (dʑ) is a documented process primarily observed in specific dialects of Modern Greek. This shift involves both a fronting of the articulation from the velar region to the alveolo-palatal region and a change in manner from a fricative to an affricate, typically triggered by a following front vowel or palatal glide.

examples.note: The underlying velar fricative /ɣ/ in words like 'γέρος' (old man) undergoes palatalization and affrication to [dʑ] before front vowels in Cretan Greek, contrasting with the palatal fricative [ʝ] in Standard Modern Greek.

sources: velar fronting in modern greek dialects
